metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly include 'extend eval_solver.run_case with a diff-context solver arm for code-review-base / overlay / verify prompt ids' in scope (or as a named dependency story), so AC3's 'specs pass on the current gate as baseline' is achievable end-to-end.
findings.finding: The plan scopes to 'Eval specs + fixtures under src/rebar/llm/evals and eval_specs' but the code-review prompt ids (code-review-base, code-review-tests, code-review-security, code-review-verify) are not handled by the existing eval_solver.run_case — it raises ValueError for any prompt_id that is neither a plan-review criterion nor one of the three hardcoded agentic ids (completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment). Code-review prompts consume a diff context (DiffContext / assemble_diff_context), not a ticket store, so a new solver arm is required before the specs can satisfy AC3 ('specs pass on the current gate as baseline').
findings.location: Scope section: 'Eval specs + fixtures under src/rebar/llm/evals and eval_specs'; AC3: 'specs pass on the current gate as baseline'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'Extend eval_solver.run_case with a code-review arm: construct a DiffContext from the case's diff_text/changed_files fixture fields and invoke the prompt via the existing structured-output path, parallel to the plan-review criterion arm.' This is a small, contained extension (similar in size to the existing ticket-based arms) that directly gates AC3.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly scope adding a code-review dispatch arm to eval_solver.run_case (or an equivalent solver injection) as a prerequisite task, or confirm the eval specs will use an alternative runner path and name it.
findings.finding: The plan implicitly assumes eval_solver.run_case can execute code-review prompts (code-review-base, code-review-verify, overlay IDs), but the actual implementation only dispatches three hardcoded agentic reviewers (completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment) plus inline plan-review criterion text. Any code-review prompt_id falls through to 'raise ValueError(f"no eval solver for prompt {prompt_id!r}")'. No code-review dispatch path exists.
findings.location: What section — 'Build eval specs for the four-pass code-review prompts (base, overlays, verify) in the existing rebar eval harness'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add eval_solver.run_case() to the plan's scope (or explicitly note it as a prerequisite): a new arm dispatching code-review prompt ids through `review_code` / `produce_code_review_verdict` with an injected runner and a diff-context fixture is required for the specs to execute in the harness.
findings.finding: eval_solver.run_case() has no arm for any code-review prompt id ('code-review-base', 'code-review-tests', 'code-review-security', 'code-review-verify') — all fall through to `raise ValueError(f"no eval solver for prompt {prompt_id!r}")`. The plan targets src/rebar/llm/evals and src/rebar/llm/eval_specs but does not enumerate eval_solver as a required update, so the new specs cannot actually execute in the harness as-written.
findings.location: src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py:106-171 (run_case); plan 'Scope' section
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'Extend eval_solver.run_case() with a code-review arm that calls review_code() (or produce_code_review_verdict directly) with the case's diff_text/changed_files fixture fields and the injected runner, analogous to the completion-verifier arm.' Or, if eval_solver is intentionally out of scope, add an AC that the new specs are offline-only (schema-validate + deterministic scorer dry-runs) with a follow-on story for live execution.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add (or plan for) a code-review dispatch arm in `eval_solver.run_case` that accepts a `diff_text` case field and routes to the code-review workflow (e.g. via `produce_code_review_verdict` with an injected FakeRunner offline), so code-review eval specs can actually run in the harness.
findings.finding: eval_solver.run_case has no dispatch for code-review prompt ids ('code-review-base', 'code-review-tests', 'code-review-security', 'code-review-verify'); it would raise ValueError for each. The plan says build specs 'in the existing rebar eval harness' and that 'specs pass on the current gate as baseline' (AC3), but the harness cannot currently run any code-review prompt — the mechanism is incomplete as described.
findings.location: What section / AC3 ('specs pass on the current gate as baseline') / src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py lines 136–171
findings.suggested_fix: In eval_solver.run_case, add an elif branch for prompt_ids matching 'code-review-*': stand up a case_store with the fixture files, then call produce_code_review_verdict(cfg, diff_text=case['diff_text'], changed_files=..., runner=runner, repo_root=root). For the verify prompt specifically, consider a standalone fixture shape that pre-injects a finding list as `instructions`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify (or confirm) how eval_solver.run_case() will be extended with a code-review arm that accepts diff-fixture cases (diff_text + changed_files + optional repo files) and routes them through produce_code_review_verdict or an equivalent isolated code-review solver path.
findings.finding: The plan asserts the new code-review eval specs will run 'in the existing rebar eval harness', but eval_solver.run_case() has no code-review arm: it handles completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment, and inline-text plan-review criteria — but nothing routes a code-review-base, code-review-tests, code-review-security, or code-review-verify case. The code-review gate takes diff context (diff_text + changed_files + an agentic file-tool repo), which is structurally different from every existing solver arm. This capability is unverified.
findings.location: What / Scope — 'Build eval specs... in the existing rebar eval harness'; also src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py (no code-review arm)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'code-review' solver arm to eval_solver.run_case() following the case_store pattern: seed fixture files into a disposable git repo, manufacture a diff via git diff, and call produce_code_review_verdict(cfg, diff_text=..., changed_files=..., runner=runner, enabled=True). Alternatively, define a lighter single-pass solver that calls the base/overlay/verify prompts directly (without the full gate wrapper) — document the chosen approach in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add (or plan to add) a `code-review` arm in `eval_solver.run_case` that calls `produce_code_review_verdict(diff_text=case['diff_text'], enabled=True, ...)` so code-review prompt ids dispatch correctly through the harness.
findings.finding: eval_solver.run_case has no arm for any code-review prompt id ('code-review-base', 'code-review-verify', 'code-review-security', 'code-review-tests', etc.), so the plan's assertion that eval specs for the four-pass code-review prompts can be built 'in the existing rebar eval harness' without modification is an unverified capability claim. The plan does not name this solver gap or commit to extending the solver.
findings.location: ## What / ## Scope — 'Build eval specs … in the existing rebar eval harness'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a branch in run_case: `if prompt_id in {'code-review-base', 'code-review-verify'} or prompt_id.startswith('code-review-'):` that calls `gate_dispatch.produce_code_review_verdict(cfg, diff_text=case.get('diff_text'), changed_files=case.get('changed_files'), enabled=True, repo_root=root)` inside a `case_store(case)` context, and maps the resulting verdict dict to a `{findings: [...]}` shape the existing scorers understand.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a code-review solver arm to `eval_solver.run_case` (or document it as a prerequisite deliverable) so the harness can dispatch `code-review-base`, `code-review-verify`, `code-review-tests`, and `code-review-security` prompt ids against diff-fixture cases.
findings.finding: The eval_solver.run_case function has no dispatch arm for any code-review prompt id. A prompt_id of 'code-review-base', 'code-review-verify', 'code-review-tests', or 'code-review-security' is not a plan-review criterion (not in plan_review registry), so every such case falls through to `raise ValueError(f'no eval solver for prompt {prompt_id!r}')`. The plan assumes the harness can run these specs but does not name extending run_case as a deliverable.
findings.location: Plan What/Scope section; src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_solver.py lines 136–171
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `code-review-*` dispatch branch to eval_solver.run_case that assembles a diff fixture (analogous to case_store for the ticket-based ops) and calls the code-review batch_runner or individual prompt ops, or explicitly list this as a scope deliverable in the plan.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Register at least one deterministic scorer (e.g. `emits_valid_code_review_base_output`, `emits_valid_code_review_findings`) in `eval_scorers.REGISTRY` that validates the `code_review_base_output` / `code_review_findings` contracts, or document which existing scorer is intentionally reused and why the shape mismatch is acceptable.
findings.finding: The plan requires deterministic gating scorers per spec (AC3), but no scorer name in the existing REGISTRY (eval_scorers.py) validates a `code_review_base_output` or `code_review_findings` structured-output contract. `emits_valid_review_result` calls `validate_result()` which validates against the `review_result` JSON schema — a shape that differs from `code_review_base_output` (which includes `recommend_overlays`) and `code_review_findings`. Using `emits_valid_review_result` on a code-review case would cause false failures; no valid alternative scorer name exists. `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` rejects unregistered scorer names.
findings.location: AC3 ('at least one deterministic gating scorer per spec'); src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py REGISTRY (lines 336–362)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the fixture shape for the code-review-verify eval spec explicitly: each case needs a pre-seeded `findings` list (the Pass-1 output, injected as `instructions`), paired with a diff, so the spec measures whether the verifier correctly upholds true findings and drops planted-false ones.
findings.finding: The Pass-2 verify prompt (code-review-verify.md) takes a pre-assembled finding list as its `instructions` input (output of the code_review_verify_inputs step) — it is structurally a second-pass verifier over prior findings, not a standalone finder over a diff. The plan lists 'verify' uniformly alongside base and overlay specs without addressing this fixture-shape difference: a verify spec needs seeded findings as inputs, not just a diff.
findings.location: What section ('eval specs for the four-pass code-review prompts (base, overlays, verify)') / AC1 ('Eval specs exist for base, verify, and at least the tests+security overlays') / src/rebar/llm/reviewers/code-review-verify.md

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define AC4 as a specific observable artifact: 'A fixture file at `src/rebar/llm/evals/fixtures/nit_framing_positive.yaml` exists, contains the measured recall-lift delta (e.g., `recall_lift: 0.15`), and is exercised by the spec's scorer.'
findings.finding: AC4 ('The nit-framing experiment result captured as a case') is effort-framed: 'captured' describes an action, not an observable artifact, and neither the case format nor the expected content of the result is specified.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'The nit-framing experiment result (positive-framing recall lift) captured as a case'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a description in the What (or a new 'Framing experiment' subsection) explaining the nit-framing / positive-framing experiment: where its result data lives, what 'captured as a case' means concretely (fixture file, expected scorer output), and which eval spec file it belongs in.
findings.finding: AC4 references a 'nit-framing experiment result (positive-framing recall lift)' that must be captured as a case, but the What, Why, and Scope sections contain zero description of this experiment, its data source, or how the result is represented — making this an orphan criterion with no described deliverable backing it.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'The nit-framing experiment result (positive-framing recall lift) captured as a case.'
findings.suggested_fix: In the What section, add: 'Framing experiment case: one recall fixture derived from the documented positive-framing A/B result (source: <link/doc>), added to the base eval spec, asserting that the positively-framed prompt variant flags the seeded defect while the nit-framed variant does/does not (per the documented result).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the nit-framing experiment (what prompt change was tested, what the recall lift was, what the fixture should look like) in the plan's What section, or link to the existing result artifact by path/ticket ID.
findings.finding: AC4 references 'the nit-framing experiment result (positive-framing recall lift)' as something to be 'captured as a case', but no experiment result, document, data file, or prior fixture by this name exists anywhere in the repo. The plan provides no description of what the experiment measured, what the result was, or where its data lives.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'The nit-framing experiment result (positive-framing recall lift) captured as a case'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the nit-framing experiment structure in the plan (or in a referenced doc): what the positive-framing variant looks like as input text, what the expected `expect` label is (e.g. `finding` / `pass`), and which scorer gates recall on it.
findings.finding: AC4 references 'the nit-framing experiment result (positive-framing recall lift) captured as a case' but no section of the plan (What, Why, Scope) defines what the experiment is, what the framing variant looks like as a fixture, what its expected label (`expect` value) is, or which scorer would gate it. The `expect` vocabulary in ALLOWED_EXPECTS has no 'nit-framing' axis; an implementer cannot construct this case without inventing the experiment design.
findings.location: AC4: 'The nit-framing experiment result (positive-framing recall lift) captured as a case'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reword AC3 to clarify what 'baseline' means when the specs are net-new (e.g., 'new specs validate cleanly and the deterministic scorer passes on a synthetic fixture'), removing the false implication that a prior passing baseline exists.
findings.finding: The plan's AC3 asserts 'specs pass on the current gate as baseline', but no code-review eval spec files (code-review-*.eval.yaml) exist anywhere in the repo today. There is no existing baseline to pass against; the assertion presupposes a state that does not hold.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'specs pass on the current gate as baseline'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'specs pass on the current gate as baseline' to 'new specs validate against the eval schema (validate_eval_spec) and each spec's deterministic scorer returns PASS on at least one seeded fixture'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Revise the 'specs pass on the current gate as baseline' AC to explicitly split the oracle contract: no-fire cases baseline = deterministic scorer asserts gate is SILENT; recall cases baseline = deterministic scorer asserts gate FIRES — and if any recall case currently fails (gate misses it), record it as a tracked regression (XFAIL/known-bad) rather than excluding it or snapshotting a miss as correct.
findings.finding: The AC 'specs pass on the current gate as baseline' risks encoding the self-authored-oracle / change-detector anti-pattern: if the current gate is miscalibrated (the explicit motivation for this work is 'uncalibrated drift'), snapshotting its output — including on seeded-defect recall cases the gate currently misses — locks in broken behavior. A recall case the gate currently misses would either be excluded to keep the baseline green, or the baseline would snapshot a miss as 'correct,' defeating the purpose of the eval.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'specs pass on the current gate as baseline'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the single blanket AC with two: (1) 'No-fire cases: all pass with gate-silent assertion on the current gate.' (2) 'Recall cases: all pass with gate-fires assertion on the current gate; any recall case the current gate misses is recorded as XFAIL with a linked follow-up ticket, not excluded.' This ensures the baseline is a behavioral oracle, not a snapshot of current (possibly wrong) behavior.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify 'current gate' by name/path and define 'pass' with a concrete threshold (e.g., 'NO-FIRE false-positive rate = 0%, SEEDED-DEFECT recall ≥ 5/6') so AC3 is deterministically evaluable.
findings.finding: 'Specs pass on the current gate as baseline' (AC3) is ambiguous: 'pass' has no numeric threshold (recall %, precision %, all-cases-pass?), and 'current gate' is not identified by name or path, so an executor cannot determine what to run or what output constitutes passing.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC3: 'specs pass on the current gate as baseline'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reframe AC2 as an observable outcome with a minimum count and schema reference, e.g.: 'At least 3 NO-FIRE and 6 SEEDED-DEFECT fixtures exist under `src/rebar/llm/evals/fixtures/`, each conforming to the rebar fixture schema, covering the named dso incidents.'
findings.finding: AC2 ('No-fire and recall case families seeded') is effort-framed: 'seeded' describes an activity, not an observable outcome, and no minimum case count or fixture schema is stated, making the criterion unmeasurable.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'No-fire and recall case families seeded from the dso incidents listed above'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and link the concrete artifact(s) that constitute 'dso's documented corpus' for code-review incidents (a file path, ticket ID, or external doc URL), or replace with 'author from scratch' and remove the sourcing claim.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Seed two families from dso's documented corpus' treating 'dso's documented corpus' as a named, locatable artifact, but no file or document in the repo constitutes a dso code-review incident corpus. The existing 'corpus: dso' fields in plan-review eval specs are metadata labels, not a data source containing the specific code-review incidents named in the plan (sourced-bash functions, tautological tests, dangling deletions, asymmetric signature changes, etc.).
findings.location: What section — 'Seed two families from dso's documented corpus'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC (e.g., the harness invocation and its expected exit code or score output) so an agent or CI step can produce machine-readable evidence of completion.
findings.finding: None of the four acceptance criteria includes a concrete proving command or check that produces evidence on success — an executor cannot verify completion without guessing how to invoke the eval harness.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all four bullet points)
findings.suggested_fix: Append a 'Verification' section listing one command per AC, e.g.: (AC1) `ls src/rebar/llm/evals/{base,verify,tests_overlay,security_overlay}.yaml` exits 0; (AC3) `rebar eval run --spec base --assert-baseline` exits 0 with scorer output showing ≥N/N cases pass.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the Scope section (or add a note to What) whether the deterministic scorers are wired into CI gating as part of this story or are defined-but-not-wired, and reconcile that statement with the 'a deterministic scorer GATES' language in What.
findings.finding: The What section declares 'a deterministic scorer GATES' while the Scope section simultaneously states 'no gate behavior change here' — creating a cross-section contradiction about whether the scorers built in this story actually enforce gating.
findings.location: What section ('Deterministic gating scorers') vs. Scope section ('no gate behavior change here')
findings.suggested_fix: If gating is deferred: change the What description to 'Deterministic scorers defined (will gate in a follow-on story); llm-judge reports only.' If gating is immediate: remove 'no gate behavior change here' from Scope and add the CI wiring step to the decomposition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an ADR or CLAUDE.md/design-doc section capturing the two-tier scorer contract (deterministic GATES, llm-judge REPORTS) and the NO-FIRE/SEEDED-DEFECT fixture-family schema so future eval contributors have a canonical reference.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a novel two-tier scorer architecture (deterministic scorer GATES vs. llm-judge only REPORTS) and a new fixture-family schema (NO-FIRE / SEEDED-DEFECT), but no ADR, CLAUDE.md update, or design-doc update is mentioned to capture this architectural decision for future maintainers.
findings.location: What section / Acceptance Criteria — 'Deterministic gating scorers (a deterministic scorer GATES; an llm-judge only REPORTS)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a brief ADR (e.g., `docs/adr/eval-scorer-tiers.md`) or a section in CLAUDE.md that defines: (1) the deterministic-gates / llm-judge-reports distinction and when each is appropriate, (2) the NO-FIRE and SEEDED-DEFECT fixture-family conventions and sourcing rules.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either extend AC1 to name all four passes explicitly, or add an explicit deferral note naming which overlay(s) are out of scope for this story and where they will be addressed.
findings.finding: The title promises eval coverage for 'four-pass code-review prompts' but AC1 only requires 'base, verify, and at least the tests+security overlays', leaving the remaining overlay pass(es) implicitly out of scope without explicit deferral acknowledgment.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1: 'Eval specs exist for base, verify, and at least the tests+security overlays'
findings.suggested_fix: Update AC1 to: 'Eval specs exist for all four passes (base, verify, tests overlay, security overlay); any overlay not yet implemented is listed as a named follow-on story in the plan.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either run the nit-framing / positive-framing experiment first and cite its documented result (a run artifact in docs/experiments/ or a calibration doc) before encoding it as a dataset case in AC4, or reframe AC4 as a TODO placeholder that is explicitly excluded from the baseline-passing requirement until the experiment is completed.
findings.finding: AC4 ('the nit-framing experiment result (positive-framing recall lift) captured as a case') references an experiment that does not exist in the codebase: no such experiment file, run artifact, or documented result appears anywhere in docs/experiments/, eval_specs/, or the codebase. Asking the spec to 'capture' a result that hasn't been produced yet is YAGNI — the case would encode a hypothetical finding, not a real measured lift.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4: 'The nit-framing experiment result (positive-framing recall lift) captured as a case.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan which registered scorer (or new scorer name to add to eval_scorers.REGISTRY) will gate the code-review-verify eval spec, given its output shape is a code_review_verdict ({blocking, advisory, coverage_gaps}) rather than a review_result or graded validity.
findings.finding: The plan names 'verify' as one of the four-pass prompts requiring an eval spec (AC1: 'Eval specs exist for base, verify, and at least the tests+security overlays'), but the verify prompt's output contract is code_review_verdict (blocking/advisory partition), not a review_result or a plan-review finding. The existing deterministic scorers in eval_scorers.REGISTRY do not include a scorer for this shape. The plan does not specify which registered scorer name will gate the verify spec.
findings.location: AC1 ('Eval specs exist for base, verify...'); src/rebar/llm/evals/eval_scorers.py:336-362 (REGISTRY)

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
